statement: Clearly Canadian has approximately 2% unaided brand awareness in the U.S. sparkling water category, indicating the brand remains largely undiscovered by new consumers despite strong nostalgia among existing fans.
source: Clearly Canadian internal brand tracker 2024
notes: Unaided awareness measures spontaneous recall without prompting.

statement: When shown the Clearly Canadian bottle, approximately 23% of U.S. adults aged 30–55 recognize the brand, representing a substantial pool of lapsed consumers with latent loyalty.
source: Clearly Canadian internal brand tracker 2024
notes: Aided awareness skews strongly toward consumers who grew up in the 1990s.

statement: Among consumers who are aware of Clearly Canadian, approximately 48% indicate they would consider purchasing it — meaning awareness itself is the primary barrier to growth, not preference once known.
source: Clearly Canadian internal conversion funnel analysis 2024
notes: High consideration-to-awareness ratio suggests strong product-market fit once consumers are reached.

statement: Awareness — not price, flavor, or distribution — is the primary barrier to trial for Clearly Canadian in the U.S. market, making brand discovery investments disproportionately high-ROI.
source: Clearly Canadian CMO interview, BevNet 2024

statement: Gen Z consumers in the U.S. and Canada most commonly discover new beverage brands through TikTok and Instagram Reels, with fridge-restock and 'what I drink in a day' content formats driving the highest engagement and trial intent.
source: eMarketer Gen Z Beverage Discovery Report 2024

statement: In-store product sampling increases trial purchase rates by 3–5x for new or returning beverage brands, making it one of the highest-conversion awareness tactics available at retail.
source: POPAI Sampling Effectiveness Meta-Study 2023

statement: Costco product demonstrations for premium beverages generate 300–500 trial units per demo day, with above-average purchase conversion due to the club's pre-qualified, value-seeking but quality-conscious shopper base.
source: Costco Supplier Demo Program internal benchmarks, reported in trade press 2023

statement: Approximately 80% of sparkling water volume growth is driven by consumers switching from carbonated soft drinks, not from water category expansion — making CSD drinkers the highest-priority acquisition target.
source: Circana U.S. Beverage Category Switching Study 2024

statement: Clearly Canadian's SleekCan SKU is priced at approximately $2.49–$2.99 USD for a single 355mL can at retail, positioning it as a premium-but-accessible option that bridges the gap between LaCroix and high-end imported sparkling water.
source: Clearly Canadian retail price audit, IRI data 2024

statement: Over 60% of single-serve sparkling water purchases at convenience stores and gas stations are unplanned, driven by cold-case visibility and thirst at the point of sale.
source: Convenience Industry Council of Canada Shopper Behavior Study 2023

statement: Household grocery purchasers buying sparkling water for family consumption overwhelmingly prefer multi-pack formats (8–12 pack) and make planned, list-driven purchase decisions — a fundamentally different buying mode than single-can impulse.
source: Nielsen Homescan Panel CPG Purchase Patterns 2023

statement: LaCroix 12-packs retail at approximately $5.99–$7.99 USD, establishing a mass-market price anchor that positions premium sparkling water brands like Clearly Canadian above $1/can as 'trading up'.
source: IRI U.S. Sparkling Water Shelf Price Audit 2024

statement: Gen Z beverage buyers strongly prefer single-can or single-bottle purchasing over multi-packs, driven by a desire to try multiple flavors, limited storage space, and lifestyle-driven consumption (rather than pantry stocking).
source: Mintel Gen Z Beverage Purchase Habits Report 2024

statement: The global sparkling water market was valued at approximately $42.8 billion in 2023 and is projected to grow at a 9–12% CAGR through 2030, driven by CSD-to-sparkling-water switching across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific.
source: Grand View Research Global Sparkling Water Market Report 2024

statement: Flavored sparkling water is the fastest-growing sub-segment of the sparkling water category, projected at approximately 12% CAGR, outpacing plain/unflavored sparkling water as consumers seek taste without calories or artificial sweeteners.
source: Mordor Intelligence Flavored Sparkling Water Market Report 2024

statement: Caffeinated sparkling water is growing at approximately 12.6% CAGR, as energy drink consumers seek cleaner alternatives and coffee drinkers look for cold, carbonated caffeine options without the calories of energy drinks.
source: Allied Market Research Caffeinated Sparkling Water Report 2024

statement: The functional beverage segment (sparkling water with added vitamins, electrolytes, adaptogens, or probiotics) grew 54% to reach approximately $9.2 billion in 2023, as consumers treat beverages as wellness delivery vehicles rather than just hydration.
source: SPINS Functional Beverage Category Report 2024

statement: Premium sparkling water as a cocktail and mocktail mixer is growing at approximately 9.2% CAGR, driven by both the sober-curious movement and elevated cocktail culture in hospitality.
source: Technomic On-Premise Mixer Trends Report 2024

statement: Millennials and Gen Z together represent approximately 42% of the U.S. population and are the primary driver of the ongoing shift from carbonated soft drinks (CSD) to sparkling water, with health, sugar avoidance, and authenticity as core motivators.
source: Beverage Digest U.S. Category Shift Analysis 2024

statement: Premium sparkling water in on-premise (restaurant, hotel, bar) channels has grown significantly as operators upgrade their beverage programs, driving new brand discovery for consumers who first encounter premium sparkling water in a hospitality setting.
source: Technomic Beverage Operator Trends Report 2023

statement: 67% of sparkling water buyers report reading ingredient labels before purchase, with 'no artificial sweeteners', 'no preservatives', and 'natural flavors' being the three most-checked claims — significantly higher label engagement than in carbonated soft drinks.
source: IFIC Foundation Food and Health Survey 2024

statement: Zero-sugar sparkling water is the fastest-growing sweetened-beverage sub-segment, with category volume growing at over 15% YoY as consumers abandon both sugary sodas and artificially-sweetened diet drinks in favor of naturally flavored options.
source: Nielsen CGA Sparkling Water Health Trends Report 2024

statement: For consumers aged 21–35, the beverage they carry or order in public functions as a lifestyle and identity signal — sparkling water brands with strong visual identity and story (vs. generic private label) command a significant social-proof premium.
source: Ipsos Beverage Identity and Self-Expression Study 2023

statement: The sober-curious movement has driven a significant increase in demand for premium non-alcoholic beverages in bar and restaurant settings, with consumers seeking options that feel equally sophisticated and intentional as cocktails.
source: NielsenIQ Sober Curious Consumer Report 2024

statement: Two-thirds of Millennial and Gen Z consumers report willingness to pay a premium for sustainably packaged products, though actual purchase behavior shows a 10–20% price ceiling before sustainability preference yields to cost considerations.
source: Nielsen Global Sustainability and Consumption Study 2023

statement: Aluminum beverage cans have a U.S. recycling rate of approximately 73%, the highest of any beverage packaging format, making them a genuinely more sustainable choice than PET plastic bottles or glass (which requires higher energy to produce).
source: Can Manufacturers Institute Sustainability Report 2023

statement: Aluminum can beverage packaging is growing at approximately 13% CAGR globally as brands and consumers shift away from single-use plastic, and as the beverage industry responds to extended producer responsibility (EPR) legislation.
source: Allied Market Research Aluminum Beverage Can Market Report 2024

statement: Clearly Canadian uses only glass bottles and aluminum cans — no plastic packaging — positioning the brand as a credible sustainability story in a category where many competitors still use PET plastic for multi-packs.
source: Clearly Canadian brand communications 2024

statement: Canadian consumer sentiment to 'buy Canadian' surged in early 2025 amid U.S.-Canada trade tensions and tariff threats, with multiple surveys showing 60–70%+ of Canadians actively seeking domestic alternatives to U.S. products.
source: Angus Reid Institute Buy Canadian Survey, March 2025
notes: Sentiment peaked in February–March 2025 amid 25% U.S. tariff threat; ongoing as of Q1 2025.

statement: The 'Canadian' country-of-origin marker signals clean, natural, and trustworthy product quality to consumers in key export markets (U.S., UK, Australia, Japan), commanding a meaningful premium in premium beverage categories.
source: Brand Finance Country of Origin Premium Study 2023

statement: Clearly Canadian's spring water is sourced from a British Columbia aquifer — a provenance story that resonates strongly with premium and health-conscious buyers who associate Pacific Northwest water sources with purity and environmental stewardship.
source: Clearly Canadian brand communications 2024

statement: Clearly Canadian's iconic bottle shape was designed to fit the curve of a baseball player's hand — a distinctly Canadian-origin design story that connects the brand to 1990s North American sports culture and differentiates it from European sparkling water bottle aesthetics.
source: Clearly Canadian brand history documentation 2024

statement: Canada has approximately 14 million TikTok users, with roughly 70% under 40 years old — making it a highly concentrated platform for reaching Millennial and Gen Z Canadian consumers with beverage content.
source: DataReportal Digital 2024 Canada Report

statement: Beverage brands with active and growing social media presence report approximately 20% higher customer acquisition rates compared to similar brands with low social engagement, with organic content outperforming paid on Gen Z audiences.
source: Sprout Social CPG Brand Benchmarks Report 2024

statement: Poppi prebiotic soda sold over $100,000 in product in the first 24 hours after a TikTok video featuring the brand went viral, demonstrating the explosive purchase velocity that social virality can create in the premium beverage category.
source: Poppi founder interview, Forbes 2023
notes: Poppi later acquired by PepsiCo for $1.65B in 2025 — TikTok virality was cited as a key growth driver.

statement: Fridge-restock content on TikTok and YouTube Shorts is a primary beverage discovery format, with viewers reporting high purchase intent when they see aspirational, aesthetically curated fridge organization videos featuring premium sparkling water brands.
source: Influencer Marketing Hub Beverage Content Trends Report 2024

statement: Bartenders are increasingly incorporating natural sweeteners — maple syrup, honey, agave nectar — into cocktail and mocktail programs, displacing simple syrup and creating pairing opportunities for naturally flavored sparkling water mixers.
source: USBG Bartender Trends Survey 2024

statement: Maple Old Fashioned cocktails (bourbon + maple syrup + bitters + sparkling water) have become a signature Canadian-inspired menu item at premium bars and restaurants, creating a natural on-premise activation opportunity for Clearly Canadian's Canadian identity story.
source: Technomic Menu Trends Report 2024

statement: Bartenders evaluating premium sparkling water mixers prioritize: (1) clean, neutral taste that doesn't compete with spirits; (2) consistent, sustained carbonation; (3) aesthetic bottle/can presentation for back-bar display; and (4) brand story that adds to the overall guest experience.
source: USBG Professional Mixer Evaluation Study 2023

statement: Sober-curious and non-drinking bar-goers frequently report frustration with limited sophisticated non-alcoholic options — being offered only soda water, juice, or soft drinks rather than premium alternatives — representing an underserved hospitality segment.
source: NielsenIQ Sober Curious On-Premise Experience Report 2024

statement: Since its 2017 reintroduction, Clearly Canadian has shipped over 50 million bottles, demonstrating significant and sustained commercial traction built substantially on the nostalgia-driven initial crowdfunding campaign and word-of-mouth among lapsed fans.
source: Clearly Canadian company communications 2024

statement: Clearly Canadian generated approximately $65.8 million in U.S. retail sales in the most recent measured period, representing approximately 39.4% year-over-year growth — significantly outpacing category averages.
source: IRI / Circana U.S. Retail Scan Data 2024, reported by Clearly Canadian

statement: Clearly Canadian's total revenue growth (including all channels and markets) is approximately 55% year-over-year, reflecting acceleration across both U.S. and Canadian retail as distribution expands and brand awareness compounds.
source: Clearly Canadian investor and partner communications 2024

statement: Clearly Canadian is currently available in approximately 33,600 retail doors across the U.S. and Canada — a rapidly expanding distribution footprint that continues to grow as the brand demonstrates velocity at existing retail partners.
source: Clearly Canadian distribution tracking 2024

statement: Clearly Canadian has achieved approximately 69% ACV (All Commodity Volume) in conventional grocery in its key markets, meaning it's available in roughly two-thirds of conventional grocery stores — a threshold that signals mainstream retail viability.
source: Clearly Canadian retail distribution data 2024

statement: Clearly Canadian's 2017 relaunch was funded by a remarkable crowdfunding campaign: 14,000 fans across 35 countries pre-ordered over 40,000 cases before a single bottle was produced — validating massive latent demand among lapsed consumers worldwide.
source: Clearly Canadian brand history, Food & Drink Magazine 2017

statement: Clearly Canadian's partnership with the Toronto Blue Jays positions the brand within Canadian sports culture and provides visibility to both core Canadian fans and U.S. audiences who follow MLB — an authentic expression of the brand's Canadian identity.
source: Clearly Canadian partnership announcement 2023

statement: Clearly Canadian's Originals flavors (Mountain Blackberry, Country Raspberry, Wild Cherry, Orchard Peach, Western Melon) represent approximately 85% of total portfolio volume, with the core glass bottle format driving the majority of nostalgia-driven repurchase.
source: Clearly Canadian internal sales mix data 2024
